
  ⎿  ✔ Advisor has reviewed the conversation and will apply the feedback

  ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   Ready to code?

   Here is Claude's plan:
  ╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌
   Idle-agent LLM task suggestion

   Context

   Why: Today, when an agent goes idle with no task source (the operator
   declared no [[task_sources]] and hap can't infer a next task from the pane's
   native todo widget), hap escalates with no_task_source and sends nothing —
   this is the load-bearing FR-011 invariant: "with no task source the plugin
   must never synthesize a prompt" (internal/domain/decide.go:164-166,263). The
   agent just sits idle until a human intervenes.

   Goal: When a new llm.generate_task_command is configured, an idle agent
   with no task source instead triggers a one-shot local-LLM call that suggests a
   task. The suggestion surfaces as a normal escalation. The operator can:
   - confirm+send (enter/y) → hap writes a per-agent tasks.md (single
   in-progress [-] item), registers it as a [[task_sources]] entry, and sends
   the task text to the agent's pane; or
   - dismiss (x) → nothing happens; or
   - if generation failed, the escalation shows the failure rationale and the
   operator retries with l (same key as the existing LLM-consult retry).

   Outcome: Idle agents get a proactive, operator-approved first task, and once
   tasks.md exists the normal declared-task flow takes over on subsequent idles
   (the completed-list template steers the agent to decide what's next) — hap never
   re-generates for that agent.

   Key design decisions (confirmed):
   - tasks.md lives in the plugin state dir: <StateDir>/tasks/<agent-name>.md
   (no repo pollution).
   - The presence of generate_task_command is itself the opt-in — no separate                                                                                                                        ↓
  ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   Claude has written up a plan and is ready to execute. Would you like to proceed?

   ❯ 1. Yes, and use auto mode
     2. Yes, manually approve edits
     3. No, refine with Ultraplan on Claude Code on the web
     4. Tell Claude what to change
        shift+tab to approve with this feedback

   ctrl+g to edit in  VS Code  · ~/.claude/plans/i-want-to-make-sorted-zebra.md
